events.text: A spool installed backwards relative to the iso flow arrow is a directional non-compliance — this matters especially for check valves, reducers, orifice plates, control valves, or any component with directionality, and it violates conformance to the approved iso drawing per B31.3's requirement that fabrication/erection match the engineering design.

events.text: Written to `/app/answer.json`: **decision: fail** — the spool's flow direction doesn't match the iso drawing's arrow, a direct nonconformance to the engineering design under ASME B31.3. Since the work is only rough-complete, the fix (reorient, re-fit-up, re-inspect) is still cheap; flagged as high risk because it could affect directional components (check valves, reducers, control valves) if it proceeds to weld-out/test undetected.
